title: Production Sports Bot
summary: My main build for over a year: a production bot for a large sports community. Live scores, rankings, news, competitions, and generated scoreboards, built and maintained solo in production.
context: I built this as an entry-level dev and learned most of the hard lessons in production: memory leaks, duplicate event storms, provider outages, interaction timeouts, queue spikes, and auth hardening.
demoHook: Flip between steady-state and stress-path views to see where this system bends first and what kept it stable.

runtimePanels.title: Ingestion Pipeline
runtimePanels.broken: Polling is too aggressive, ESPN responses duplicate, and request volume spikes past safe limits.
runtimePanels.fixed: Rate-aware ingestion + retry backoff + request dedup keeps updates fast without API thrash.

runtimePanels.title: Dispatch and Queueing
runtimePanels.broken: Alert bursts pile up in queue, delivery drifts, and users see late or noisy updates.
runtimePanels.fixed: Bull-backed job queues smooth burst traffic and keep delivery timing predictable.

runtimePanels.title: Provider Failure Recovery
runtimePanels.broken: Single ESPN dependency causes hard downtime during provider incidents.
runtimePanels.fixed: Circuit breaker + fallback data path keeps the bot alive and reduces pager pressure.

codeReceipts.title: Sharp Must Load First
codeReceipts.snippet:
// Sharp config MUST be imported before any other modules — each instance creates independent caches
import './utils/sharpConfig.js';

codeReceipts.title: Health Endpoint
codeReceipts.snippet: this.app.get("/health", (req, res) => { res.status(200).json({ status: "healthy", uptime: process.uptime() }); });

codeReceipts.title: Scheduler Stale Detection
codeReceipts.snippet: if (age !== null && age > maxSilence) { return { ...entry, status: entry.status === "critical" ? "critical" : "degraded", stale: true }; }

architectureNotes: Startup is split into three phases: bootstrap services, initialize core systems, then connect runtime and schedulers. This made boot failures locatable.
Core is separated into bot-core, bot-events, bot-schedulers, and bot-server so a failure in one doesn't cascade.
Image rendering uses Sharp, @resvg/resvg-js, and satori. Sharp config loads before any other module — the hard way proved each instance creates its own cache.
An ML-based news classifier (93K) categorizes and filters incoming stories. A separate adaptive classifier handles spam detection.
API access goes through a central optimization layer with caching, batching, retry normalization, and per-source rate-limit control.
14 automated schedulers run through a BotSchedulers orchestrator with heartbeat tracking and stale-threshold detection.
reliabilityNotes: Redis + in-memory fallback for cache, session, and rate-limiter paths — degraded mode beats a hard miss.
Circuit breaker services protect ESPN and other external dependencies with fallback behavior.
Scheduler health tracks heartbeat age, degraded state, and critical state — 'is it running' wasn't enough.
Graceful shutdown cleans intervals, queues, Redis connections, analytics, and process resources — 12+ components.
Dashboard auth includes role permissions, session validation, CSRF token handling, and timing-safe credential comparisons.
Platform-aware memory management: Railway runs with --expose-gc and a 10-minute GC interval; thresholds and cleanup intervals differ per platform.
postmortem: GuildPresences was enabled by default and silently caching 500MB–1GB of Discord presence data before I noticed. Disabling one intent flag freed it all.
Sharp doesn't share cache limits across instances. Image memory kept climbing until I found that every file importing Sharp made its own cache. Global config now loads before anything else.
Discord interaction timeouts (error 10062) kept appearing because I wasn't deferring fast enough. BaseCommand now defers immediately before any processing.
Graceful shutdown was an afterthought. On early restarts, intervals kept running and connections stayed open. Cleaning up 12+ components on exit fixed the instability.

tradeoffs.label: Fast polling
tradeoffs.benefit: Lower score delay during active game windows.
tradeoffs.cost: More rate-limit pressure and larger blast radius when retries misbehave.

tradeoffs.label: Strict dedupe rules
tradeoffs.benefit: Cuts duplicate spam and protects trust.
tradeoffs.cost: Overly strict fingerprints can suppress rare but valid updates.

tradeoffs.label: SVG scoreboards
tradeoffs.benefit: High-quality visual output that scans fast in Discord.
tradeoffs.cost: Image generation adds memory and render-time overhead that has to be managed carefully.

checkpoints.move: Shipped the first live ingest and Discord delivery loop on Railway.
checkpoints.signal: Core loop worked. Duplicates and noisy retries showed up immediately under real load.

checkpoints.move: Added event fingerprinting and Bull-backed queues to control burst traffic.
checkpoints.signal: Duplicate alerts dropped. Message ordering became predictable.

checkpoints.move: Shipped visual score rendering and built out the scheduler orchestration layer.
checkpoints.signal: Scoreboards landed well. Found Sharp memory issues in the process.

checkpoints.move: Implemented circuit breaker behavior, fallback data paths, and graceful shutdown.
checkpoints.signal: Provider outages no longer caused full downtime. Restarts became stable.

checkpoints.move: Expanded dashboard auth, scheduler health tracking, and memory management.
checkpoints.signal: Debug cycles got faster. The GuildPresences and Sharp issues got found and fixed.

title: Trek Field Guide
summary: A visual side project where I pushed a strong theme without letting navigation or readability collapse.
context: I used this to sharpen UI craft while my main energy stayed on the bot. It is my cleanest visual-focused build.
demoHook: Compare the calmer path and the overloaded path to see where style helps exploration and where it gets in the way.

runtimePanels.title: Navigation Density
runtimePanels.broken: Too many competing visual anchors slow down wayfinding.
runtimePanels.fixed: Clear section anchors and spacing hierarchy restore scan speed.

runtimePanels.title: Theme Weight
runtimePanels.broken: Heavy thematic surfaces overpower task-focused content.
runtimePanels.fixed: Constrained style tokens preserve personality without losing legibility.

runtimePanels.title: Interaction Rhythm
runtimePanels.broken: Motion timing stacks and adds visual fatigue.
runtimePanels.fixed: Reduced motion cadence keeps flow intentional and easier to follow.

postmortem: Wayfinding had to stay strict or the theme got in the way.
Reducing component variants made iteration faster.

tradeoffs.label: Heavy thematic styling
tradeoffs.cost: Readability degrades fast if the structure underneath is weak.

tradeoffs.label: Exploration-first layout
tradeoffs.cost: Task-focused users may prefer flatter paths.

checkpoints.move: Locked visual language early and constrained component options.
checkpoints.signal: The site felt cohesive rather than experimental noise.

checkpoints.move: Added stronger anchors and clearer section transitions.
checkpoints.signal: Target content was easier to locate.

checkpoints.move: Compared compact, balanced, and expanded layouts.
checkpoints.signal: Balanced mode provided the best readability-to-style ratio.

checkpoints.move: Removed low-signal visual flourishes during break/fix passes.

codeReceipts.title: Motion Budget Rule
codeReceipts.snippet: transition-duration: var(--motion-fast); /* reduced on dense views */
